steps: Log in to your Greenhouse account (requires Site Admin with 'Can manage ALL organization's API Credentials' developer permission)
Enter a name for the key (typically the integration name)
Configure permissions for the specific endpoints this key will access
Click 'Manage Permissions' then 'I have stored the API key' — the key is shown only once
Copy the key before confirming; Base64-encode it with a colon appended for HTTP Basic auth (e.g., base64('<key>:'))

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Harvest API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Greenhouse Harvest API key, used with HTTP Basic auth. Send as 'Basic <base64(key:)>' in the Authorization header.

notes: Requires a Greenhouse Recruiting account — contact sales for pricing
Only Site Admins with the 'Can manage ALL organization's API Credentials' permission can create keys
The API key is shown only once — store it securely before clicking 'I have stored the API key'
Authentication uses HTTP Basic auth: base64-encode '<key>:' (key followed by colon, no password)
Harvest API v1 and v2 are deprecated after August 31, 2026 — use v3 for new integrations
Permissions can be scoped per endpoint (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE) for least-privilege access

test_endpoint.description: List Greenhouse users to verify the API key and permissions are correctly configured
